clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall develop the API according to the requirements documented in [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT]. This document forms the contractual baseline for the Deliverables.
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.
The Client shall test the API against [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] within [ACCEPTANCE_PERIOD_DAYS] days of delivery. If the Client does not respond within this period, the API shall be deemed accepted.
The Service Provider shall document each endpoint's request format, response format, and error behavior in [API_DOCUMENTATION], covering the endpoints described in [ENDPOINT_LIST].
The API shall use [AUTHENTICATION_MECHANISM] to authenticate requests.

The Service Provider shall version the API according to [VERSIONING_SCHEME]. Breaking changes shall only be introduced in a new version, and the Service Provider shall provide [DEPRECATION_NOTICE_PERIOD_DAYS] days' notice before retiring a prior version.
Where the Client requests a security audit, the Service Provider shall arrange for a third-party audit of the API covering [SECURITY_AUDIT_SCOPE] for an additional fee of [SECURITY_AUDIT_FEE].
The technical constraints underlying [REQUIREMENTS_DOCUMENT] are described in [TECHNICAL_REQUIREMENTS_REFERENCE].
The Service Provider shall deliver technical documentation covering [DOCUMENTATION_SCOPE] alongside the final Deliverable.
The Service Provider shall perform quality assurance testing covering [QA_TEST_SCOPE] before delivering the API.
Where the Client requests ongoing maintenance following delivery, the Service Provider shall provide it for a fee agreed separately between the Parties.
Where the Client requests API monitoring, the Service Provider shall monitor [MONITORING_SCOPE] and report incidents to the Client within [INCIDENT_NOTIFICATION_PERIOD_HOURS] hours of detection.
Where the Client requests technical support beyond the Warranty period, the Service Provider shall provide it at [SUPPORT_HOURLY_RATE] per hour.
Where the API requires defined uptime commitments, the Service Provider shall target availability of at least [UPTIME_TARGET_PERCENT].

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.
The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE].
